Search results show multiple distance options offered: 50 km, 25 km, 15 km, and 5 km on the first day (freestyle), and 50 km, 25 km, 15 km on the second day (classic style). The race is not a single fixed distance of 50 km.
The race includes both freestyle (first day) and classic style (second day) disciplines, not skate only.

The official website describes Vasaloppet Japan as 'クロスカントリースキー及び歩くスキーの国際大会' (cross-country ski and walking ski international competition), indicating both classic and leisure/walking ski disciplines, not skate only.
2012: 47 starters for 60km classic (close to 50 within ±20% tolerance but sources show higher total; specific 60km classic matches closely)[1]
36th edition in 2018 implies first in 1983 (2018 - 36 + 1 = 1983)[2]

Search results show the 40th Viru Maraton (January 17, 2026) had distances of 35km and 18km due to weather shortening. However, the standard/planned distances are 42km and 21km according to multiple sources. The claim of 42km matches the planned full distance, but recent races have been shortened. Most recent confirmed result shows 36km as the actual race distance on 17-01-2026.
Search results indicate 'Free Technique' (FT) not 'classic'. The Worldloppet results table shows Viru Maraton races as 'FT' (Free Technique), which allows both classic and skate techniques.
